data.content:
The build confirms the issue: the file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The problem is that the `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` ItemGroup, so MSBuild doesn't include it in compilation.

Fix: add the generated file to `Compile` items within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild compile item list is already finalized by the time the file is generated.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to include the generated `.cs` file. This way, the compiler will pick it up.

I need to add:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

after the `WriteCodeFile` task call within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to MSBuild's `Compile` item group. The compiler only compiles files it knows about.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside an `<ItemGroup>` within the target, so the generated file is included in compilation.
